name: npm-release
description: Use when ready to publish a new version of cc-devflow npm package to npm registry
capabilities: Guides through semantic versioning (patch, minor, major)
Ensures version sync across package.json, CHANGELOG.md, and git tags
Provides step-by-step git operations for release commits
Includes dry-run testing before actual npm publish
Documents rollback procedures for failed releases
author: Dimon94
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing release workflow instructions. The static analyzer flagged shell command examples and URLs in documentation as security threats, but these are false positives - the skill contains no executable code, no scripts, and makes no actual network or filesystem calls. All 67 detected patterns are benign documentation elements.
valueStatement: Manual npm releases often lead to version mismatches between package.json, git tags, and changelogs. This skill provides a step-by-step workflow that keeps all version markers in perfect sync for safe, consistent publishing.
userTitle: Automate npm releases with version sync
limitations: Requires main branch with clean working directory
Does not support pre-release or beta versions

useCases.title: Release new features safely
useCases.description: Publish minor version updates with proper changelog documentation and git tagging

useCases.title: Fix and release patches
useCases.description: Quickly release bug fixes while maintaining version consistency across all markers

useCases.title: Standardize release process
useCases.description: Establish consistent release workflow across team members

promptTemplates.title: Release patch version
promptTemplates.prompt: Walk me through releasing a patch version for cc-devflow. Current version is 2.4.3 and I have fixed a critical bug in the checklist validation.

promptTemplates.title: Release minor version
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to release version 2.5.0 with new TDD enforcement features. Guide me through the complete workflow.

promptTemplates.title: Check release readiness
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me verify that my repository is ready for release. Check git status, current version, and recent commits.

promptTemplates.title: Rollback failed release
promptTemplates.prompt: I accidentally published a broken version 2.4.5. How do I unpublish it and rollback the git changes?

bestPractices: Always run npm publish --dry-run first to catch packaging issues before publishing
Update CHANGELOG.md before committing version changes to maintain documentation accuracy
Use annotated git tags with descriptive release messages for better traceability
Verify package installation works after publishing to confirm successful release
antiPatterns: Never push git tags before committing version changes - causes version mismatch
Do not skip changelog updates for any release - breaks traceability
Avoid manual version editing without using npm version command - causes errors

faq.question: Is this compatible with other npm packages?
faq.answer: The workflow is generic but examples reference cc-devflow. Adapt the package name and repository URLs for your project.

faq.question: What if npm publish fails due to network issues?
faq.answer: The workflow includes network troubleshooting steps. Retry after connection stabilizes or switch to SSH for git operations.

faq.question: Can I use this for beta or pre-release versions?
faq.answer: The current workflow focuses on stable releases. Pre-release versions need additional npm tag configuration.

faq.question: How do I handle release failures after 72 hours?
faq.answer: After 72 hours, npm unpublish is unavailable. Publish a new patch version with the fix instead of unpublishing.

faq.question: Does this integrate with CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: This is a manual workflow. For CI/CD integration, you will need additional automation scripts and npm tokens.

faq.question: What permissions do I need?
faq.answer: npm publish permission for the package, git push access to the repository, and optionally GitHub CLI for release creation.
